message.content:
You are verifier #2, independent of all other verifiers. Do NOT confirm findings optimistically — only confirm if you can independently verify the issue exists in the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.

Finding F-026: "Concurrent test and single-threaded tests mutate os.environ directly without monkeypatch"
File: tests/test_persistence.py (line 476)
Description: Tests call os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = ... directly, bypassing monkeypatch. Single-threaded tests leave ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL permanently mutated, polluting subsequent tests.
PoC: Run two tests sequentially that set os.environ directly. Second test sees first test's value if monkeypatch.delenv not called.

Instructions:
1. Read the actual file at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_persistence.py
2. Look at the code at line 476 and surrounding context
3. Independently verify whether the described problem EXISTS in the current code
4. Check if the PoC is reproducible from the actual code as written
5. Consider whether this is already fixed (pass-1 #101-#122, pass-2 #124-#156 fixed many issues)
6. Return your independent verdict

Confirmed = true ONLY if: (a) the code problem exists as described, (b) the PoC is reproducible from current code, (c) it is a genuine production-readiness concern. Set confirmed=false for speculative issues, issues already fixed, or issues where the PoC does not match what the code actually does.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

464	def test_migration_lock_is_bounded(fake_pg):
465	    # #129: the advisory lock must be acquired under a bounded lock_timeout so a stuck peer
466	    # holding it cannot hang bootstrap forever. set_config('lock_timeout', ...) runs before the
467	    # pg_advisory_lock on the same connection.
468	    cp, state = fake_pg
469	    cp.get_checkpointer()
470	    assert state["lock_timeout_on"], "no lock_timeout set before advisory lock (#129)"
471	    # the configured bound is a positive integer-millisecond value
472	    (params,) = state["lock_timeout_on"]
473	    assert int(params[0]) > 0
474	
475	
476	def test_concurrent_init_of_distinct_conn_strs_does_not_serialize(fake_pg, monkeypatch):
477	    # #129: a stuck advisory-lock wait on conn_A must NOT block bootstrap of an unrelated conn_B.
478	    # We make conn_A's advisory lock block on an Event; conn_B (different conn_str) must still
479	    # complete. The pre-#129 code held a single process-global mutex across the advisory wait, so
480	    # conn_B would be blocked behind conn_A — this test discriminates that regression.
481	    import os
482	    import sys
483	    import threading
484	
485	    cp, state = fake_pg
486	    release_a = threading.Event()
487	    a_in_lock = threading.Event()
488	
489	    BlockPool = sys.modules["psycopg_pool"].ConnectionPool
490	
491	    class BlockingConn:
492	        def __init__(self, conn_str):
493	            self.conn_str = conn_str
494	
495	        def execute(self, sql, params=None):
496	            if "pg_advisory_lock" in sql and "5432/assay" in self.conn_str:
497	                a_in_lock.set()
498	                release_a.wait(5)  # hold the lock until released
499	
500	    class _PoolWrap(BlockPool):
501	        def __init__(self, conn_str, **kw):
502	            super().__init__(conn_str, **kw)
503	            self._conn = BlockingConn(conn_str)
504	
505	    monkeypatch.setattr(sys.modules["psycopg_pool"], "ConnectionPool", _PoolWrap)
506	
507	    results: dict = {}
508	
509	    def init_a():
510	        os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
511	        cp.get_checkpointer()
512	        results["a"] = True
513	
514	    def init_b():
515	        a_in_lock.wait(5)  # ensure A is parked inside its advisory-lock wait
516	        os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = "postgresql://localhost:5433/other_db"
517	        cp.get_checkpointer()
518	        results["b"] = True
519

519	
520	    ta = threading.Thread(target=init_a)
521	    tb = threading.Thread(target=init_b)
522	    ta.start()
523	    tb.start()
524	    tb.join(5)
525	    assert results.get("b") is True, "conn_B blocked behind conn_A's advisory wait (#129)"
526	    release_a.set()
527	    ta.join(5)
528	    assert results.get("a") is True
529

352	def fake_pg(monkeypatch):
353	    """Inject fake langgraph/psycopg modules so get_checkpointer runs the full hardened path
354	    offline, and reset the module's process-local bootstrap state."""
355	    import sys
356	    import types
357	
358	    from assay_engine.persistence import checkpoint as cp
359	
360	    state = {
361	        "setup_on": [],
362	        "lock_on": [],
363	        "lock_timeout_on": [],
364	        "pools": [],
365	        "atexit_registered": 0,
366	    }
367	
368	    class FakeConn:
369	        def execute(self, sql, params=None):
370	            if "lock_timeout" in sql:
371	                state["lock_timeout_on"].append(params)
372	            if "pg_advisory_lock" in sql:
373	                state["lock_on"].append(id(self))
374	
375	    class FakePool:
376	        check_connection = staticmethod(lambda *a, **k: None)
377	
378	        def __init__(self, conn_str, **kw):
379	            self.conn_str = conn_str
380	            self._conn = FakeConn()
381	            state["pools"].append(self)
382	
383	        def connection(self):
384	            conn = self._conn
385	
386	            class _Ctx:
387	                def __enter__(self_):
388	                    return conn
389	
390	                def __exit__(self_, *a):
391	                    return False
392	
393	            return _Ctx()
394	
395	        def close(self):
396	            pass
397	
398	    class FakeSaver:
399	        def __init__(self, arg):
400	            self.arg = arg
401	
402	        def setup(self):
403	            state["setup_on"].append(id(self.arg))
404	
405	    def _mod(name, **attrs):
406	        m = types.ModuleType(name)
407	        for k, v in attrs.items():
408	            setattr(m, k, v)
409	        monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, name, m)
410	        return m
411	
412	    _mod("langgraph")
413	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint")
414	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.postgres", PostgresSaver=FakeSaver)
415	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.memory", MemorySaver=type("MemorySaver", (), {}))
416	    _mod("psycopg")
417	    _mod("psycopg.rows", dict_row=object())
418	    _mod("psycopg_pool", ConnectionPool=FakePool)
419	
420	    # reset process-local bootstrap bookkeeping
421	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS", set())
422	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_OPEN_POOLS", [])
423	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_atexit_registered", False)
424	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_CONN_INIT_LOCKS", {})
425	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_POOLS_BY_CONN", {})
426	    monkeypatch.setattr(
427	        cp,
428	        "atexit",
429	        types.SimpleNamespace(
430	            register=lambda fn: state.__setitem__(
431	                "atexit_registered", state["atexit_registered"] + 1
432	            )
433	        ),
434	    )
435	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL", "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay")
436	    return cp, state

content.text:
I have a complete picture of the issue. The finding is accurate. Here is what the code shows:

1. The `fake_pg` fixture at line 435 uses `monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL", ...)` which properly tracks and restores the value after the test.

2. However, `test_pool_is_cached_and_reused_per_conn_str` (line 449) calls `os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = "postgresql://localhost:5433/other"` directly (line 459) — no monkeypatch, no cleanup.

3. `test_concurrent_init_of_distinct_conn_strs_does_not_serialize` (line 476) uses `monkeypatch` as a parameter but its thread functions `init_a` (line 510) and `init_b` (line 516) call `os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = ...` directly from within threads. `monkeypatch.setenv` is not thread-safe (it's not even used here), so the values mutated in the threads are not tracked for teardown.

4. `test_atexit_pool_cleanup_registered_once` (line 531) calls `os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = "postgresql://localhost:5433/second"` directly (line 536) — again, no monkeypatch, no cleanup.

These direct mutations leak into subsequent tests. The `fake_pg` fixture does set the env var via monkeypatch (which will restore to the pre-fixture value after the test), but the direct `os.environ` mutations within the test body happen AFTER the fixture's `monkeypatch.setenv` snapshot, so monkeypatch will restore to the value from before the fixture ran — not necessarily a clean state if the previous test left a residual value that wasn't cleaned up. More critically, threads that set `os.environ` directly are definitely not tracked by monkeypatch at all.

input.reasoning:
The issue exists in the current code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_persistence.py. Three test functions mutate os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] directly without monkeypatch:

1. Line 459 in test_pool_is_cached_and_reused_per_conn_str: os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = "postgresql://localhost:5433/other" — no cleanup, no monkeypatch.

2. Lines 510 and 516 in test_concurrent_init_of_distinct_conn_strs_does_not_serialize: both thread functions (init_a and init_b) call os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = ... directly. Although monkeypatch is a parameter of the outer test, it is not used to set these values; thread-body mutations are not tracked for teardown.

3. Line 536 in test_atexit_pool_cleanup_registered_once: os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = "postgresql://localhost:5433/second" — again direct mutation with no cleanup.

The fake_pg fixture does set the env var via monkeypatch.setenv (line 435), which will restore to the pre-fixture snapshot on teardown. However, the direct os.environ mutations that happen inside the test body after the fixture runs are not tracked by monkeypatch. The thread-body mutations are guaranteed not tracked. After these tests complete, ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL may be left set to a stale value ("postgresql://localhost:5433/other", "postgresql://localhost:5433/other_db", or "postgresql://localhost:5433/second") depending on test ordering and scheduling. Any subsequent test that reads ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL without explicitly setting it (e.g., via a new monkeypatch.setenv call) will observe a polluted environment. This is a genuine production-readiness concern for test isolation and CI reliability.
